target.name: Vertical Semiconductor
target.founder: Joshua Perozek, Cynthia Liao, Tomas Palacios

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vertical Semiconductor is a Seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Joshua Perozek, Cynthia Liao, and Tomas Palacios that has raised $11M to date. The valuation exercise draws on a comp universe of 11 peers across Chips & Semiconductors—3 public (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private entities including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, and Etched.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised ($11M) as a proxy for current value and applying a standard 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next stage yields $28M. A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-set comparables; while 10 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints, making sector-wide medians unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $23M to $32M, centered on $28M. This range reflects the inherent uncertainty of single-method triangulation and the absence of directly comparable early-stage semiconductor companies. Key risk factors include the company's reliance on capital intensity typical of the sector, stage-progression assumptions that may not reflect market conditions at Series A, and the thinness of early-stage semiconductor financing data. Investors should weight execution risk and market receptivity to the company's specific technical approach accordingly.
